selected_important_turn.text: ok, but i want 2 pack of food, 2 pack of water and 1 pack of firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the first concrete, self-favoring proposal after the opponent explicitly said water is their top need. It shifts the interaction into direct competition over water and therefore strongly shapes the rest of the negotiation (escalates zero-sum framing).
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is rigid and self-centered given the opponent's stated need; it neither acknowledges their reason nor tries to find tradeoffs. That locks the convo into a contested split instead of opening integrative options (e.g., conditional trades, quantity clarification). Because it's an explicit offer, small changes here would have high leverage on fairness and the chance of a mutually beneficial solution.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Move from a blunt self-favoring allocation toward an explicitly conditional or integrative proposal: acknowledge their water need, ask/confirm the quantity they must have, and offer tradeoffs (e.g., if you take X water, I need Y food or trade another concession). Use concrete quantities but leave room for exchange rather than staking an uncompromising claim.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (creates room for logrolling and more efficient splits)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (acknowledges the other’s need and balances claims)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (clear conditional offers reduce resistance and misunderstanding)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (showing recognition of the other party’s need builds trust)

value2reason.High: it is most required to health

value2reason.Low: I need more food as I have more children attending than expected
value2reason.Medium: It iis very cold at night and I need firewood to keep warm
value2reason.High: I need a lot o drinking water  to hydrate as I will be doing hard activities.

dialogue.text: hello
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hi friend. How is your day?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: yes good, what you want?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I want a lot of water my friend. I will be hiking and what I hav is not enough
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: ok, but i want 2 pack of food, 2 pack of water and 1 pack of firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m willing to trade 1 food for 1 water. That way, I get more water for my hikes and you get some food for your kids. How does that sound?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand you need more water. How many packs do you need at minimum? If you take 2 water, I can offer 1 food in exchange, but I still need at least 1 water for myself.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 water packs for my hikes. If you’re okay with 2 water and 1 food, I can give you 1 firewood. That way, I get enough water and you get some food for your kids.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 1 water for myself, but I’m willing to trade 1 food for 1 water if you take 2. Let’s see if we can find a fair split that works for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 water packs for my hikes. If you’re okay with 2 water and 1 food, I can give you 1 firewood. That way, I get enough water and you get some food for your kids.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 1 water for myself, but if you take 2, I can offer 1 food in exchange. Let’s split the remaining 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood evenly. That way, we both get what we need without losing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
